what_it_does: Records a channel attachment entry that links a message in the design-assets channel to an image asset target.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter with no body content.
standouts: The target uses an 'image://' URI scheme.
The file is purely metadata with no explanatory text.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with key-value metadata.
Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone.
Uses a typed attachment_type field and a URI-style target.
concerns: No message_id or author is present, so the originating channel message is not identifiable from this file alone.
The 'image://' URI cannot be resolved without additional context.
notes: This is a bare attachment ledger record tying a design-assets channel entry to a specific icon image asset.
questions: Which channel message or thread does this attachment belong to?
Where is the actual image file stored relative to the 'image://' URI?
Is 'images' the only attachment_type used for this asset?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record an attachment, and the file does exactly that by linking a channel to an image target.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a persistent, typed record of when a design image asset was attached to a channel conversation.
novelty: The use of an 'image://' URI scheme to reference a design asset target rather than a filesystem path.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed link between a channel and a named image asset, supporting asset provenance and retrieval in a fused channel-memory system.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-only channel attachment record with id att-91388383 linking the design-assets channel to the image asset image://design-assets/icons/Icon_Gold_Easy_Setup, added on 2026-06-22.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to record that an image asset was attached to a design-assets channel message at a specific time.
